seoTitle: How to Create a Smart Rich Filter in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to create a Smart Rich Filter in Jira, connect it to saved filters, and add it to dashboards for better reporting.
intro: Building interactive Jira dashboards depends on having filters that do more than a basic search. This guide covers creating a Smart Rich Filter, connecting it to your existing saved filters, and adding it to a dashboard gadget.

steps.heading: Create a Smart Rich Filter
steps.body: Start the filter creation process from your dashboard or filters area, giving it a name that reflects what it's tracking.

steps.heading: Connect saved filters
steps.body: Link the Smart Rich Filter to your existing saved filters so it can pull from queries you've already built.

steps.heading: Configure smart filtering options
steps.body: Adjust the smart filtering settings to control how dynamic the filter behaves and what conditions it applies.

steps.heading: Add dashboard gadgets
steps.body: Insert the Rich Filter gadget onto your dashboard so the filtered results are visible alongside your other reporting widgets.

faq.question: What is a Smart Rich Filter in Jira?
faq.answer: It's an enhanced filter type that connects to saved filters and supports dynamic filtering options for more interactive dashboards.

faq.question: How do I connect a Smart Rich Filter to a saved filter?
faq.answer: During setup, link the Smart Rich Filter to one of your existing saved filters so it can build on that saved query.

faq.question: Can I add a Smart Rich Filter to a dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, once created you can add it as a Rich Filter gadget on any dashboard you have edit access to.

faq.question: Who typically uses Smart Rich Filters in Jira?
faq.answer: Jira administrators, Scrum Masters, and project managers commonly use them to build more interactive reports for their teams.
